title: Comparative Genocide Systems Lab

fields.source_utilization: Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. Comparative genocide, atrocity, and mass-violence analysis. Use source library atrocity, law, trafficking, and dehumanization sources with a high-stakes citation ceiling. Vision: Keep atrocity and mass-violence pages ethically limited and analytically disciplined. Mechanism: Extract dehumanization, perpetration, trafficking, radicalization, humanitarian law, and accountability mechanisms. Plan: Do not strengthen public claims here without passage-level review. Status: source library metadata-selected with author correction; high-stakes passage verification pending. Remaining gap: Needs strict source linkage and public-safety/sensitivity review. Source pool: 123 deduplicated local title candidates; 129 catalogue support rows. Examples: It Can Happen Here; Perpetrators. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors where available. This strengthens page vision, mechanism...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. When to reach for this: You are building a comparative-genocide research page inside MuranoOS that must preserve the ten canonical dimensions and the settled/unsettled distinction rigorously, and you need a room that doubles as an analytic lab and a sprint-planning campaign table. Entry point: README.md — docs/01_conceptual_spec.md — handoff_manifest.json — db/001_create_genocide_lab_tables.sql — scaffold/src/rooms/genocide-lab/GenocideLabRoom.tsx. Integration target: MuranoOS desktop app. Note caveat: the room-registration stub name, command names, and runtime symbol references are all design assumptions — verify against live MuranoOS before wiring. Wire or preserve the implementation files: React/TypeScript scaffold under scaffold/src/rooms/genocide-lab/: GenocideLabRoom.tsx shell plus seven components (CampaignTable, CaseDetailPanel, CaseLibrary, DimensionLibrary, MethodChamber, Observatory, RoomDashboard). Zustand store (useGenocideLabStore.ts), useRoomCommands hook, filters.ts / selectors.ts helpers, types.ts domain model. Hub registration stub at src/hub/roomRegistry.genocideLab.stub.ts. Rust/Tauri backend: src-tauri/src/commands/genocide_lab.rs command module; mod.rs registration; src-tauri/tests/genocide_lab_smoke.rs integration smoke test.
fields.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: This package transforms a previously-dispersed comparative-genocide research program into an engineering-ready MuranoOS room specification. Its core design principle is "nothing exists in isolation; every object must link to dimensions, cases, methods, evidence, process, or output work products." The room is engineered to preserve, losslessly, the exact ten canonical analytic dimensions, the distinction between concept and operational rule, the distinction between settled and unsettled claims, the provenance of major judgments, and the comparability of cases. The package explicitly tags its assumptions: [CONFIRMED] grounded in conversation, [INFERRED] required by project logic, [ASSUMED] engineering assumption based on MuranoOS protocol, [CONTRADICTED] reserved for known conflicts (none remain open). This provenance discipline makes the handoff safe for an engineering team that did not witness the originating conversation.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: This package transforms a previously-dispersed comparative-genocide research program into an engineering-ready MuranoOS room specification.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It gives mass-violence analysis a comparative systems surface: cases can be compared through mechanisms, warning signs, institutions, rhetoric, and escalation pathways rather than treated as isolated atrocities.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: blueprint detailed source fields.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.
